title: Institution-Grade Computing Capstone: Evidence, Impact, Operations, and Defense
description: Graduating learners ready to synthesize the full platform into a consequential, independently governed capstone whose technical, human, ethical, operational, and impact claims survive changed cases and an unseen defense. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate a gated capstone from mastery contract and significant problem evidence through ethics, standards, outcome evaluation, feasibility, roadmap, architecture, domain invariants, security and privacy, cumulative construction, integration resilience, layered human evaluation, operations, trusted release, clean recovery, retirement, public documentation, and unseen defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate competency evidence that distinguishes personal work, assistance, collaboration, automated structure, runtime behavior, affected-user evidence, controlled operations, and remaining non-claims through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five defense milestones and a cumulative unfamiliar examination suitable for institution-grade completion review through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves the prior problem, stakeholder, affected-user, accessibility, security, privacy, evidence, revision, test, operation, recovery, contribution, and non-claim boundaries before adding complexity.

Browser labs analyze deterministic original evidence manifests only. They never execute learner shell commands, contact research participants or external services, deploy software, access secrets, or claim live accessibility, security, privacy, load, recovery, legal, or production behavior.

Passing work requires a prediction, traceable artifact, deterministic verification, changed and rejected cases, causal diagnosis, repair evidence, affected-user or stakeholder evidence where appropriate, explicit limitations, and an accountable owner.

competencies.statement: Map every claimed competency to prerequisite evidence, new transfer task, artifact, verification, counterexample, and defense question.
misconceptions: One complex artifact automatically demonstrates every skill used to create it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map every claimed competency to prerequisite evidence, new transfer task, artifact, verification, counterexample, and defense question.

competencies.statement: Declare prior code, templates, libraries, collaborators, generated assistance, external services, and personal contribution with verification boundaries.
misconceptions: Disclosing tools weakens a capstone and should be minimized.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Declare prior code, templates, libraries, collaborators, generated assistance, external services, and personal contribution with verification boundaries.

competencies.statement: Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion.
misconceptions: A large quantity of screenshots creates strong evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion.

competencies.statement: Predefine changed-case, causal diagnosis, tradeoff, ethics, accessibility, security, operations, and recovery questions that expose shallow mastery.
misconceptions: A prepared product walkthrough is an adequate mastery examination.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predefine changed-case, causal diagnosis, tradeoff, ethics, accessibility, security, operations, and recovery questions that expose shallow mastery.

competencies.statement: Set proposal, implementation, validation, and defense gates that can require repair, rescope, defer, or stop before credential claims.
misconceptions: Capstone evaluators should help every started project reach approval.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set proposal, implementation, validation, and defense gates that can require repair, rescope, defer, or stop before credential claims.

competencies.statement: Triangulate direct research, domain artifacts, credible data, existing services, and counterevidence to establish problem significance.
misconceptions: Novel technology makes the addressed problem significant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Triangulate direct research, domain artifacts, credible data, existing services, and counterevidence to establish problem significance.

competencies.statement: Bound affected populations, contexts, frequency, severity, unequal burden, and who is missing from available evidence.
misconceptions: A broad phrase such as everyone establishes product reach.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound affected populations, contexts, frequency, severity, unequal burden, and who is missing from available evidence.

competencies.statement: Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options.
misconceptions: Competitor feature lists explain why people use current alternatives.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options.

competencies.statement: Connect intervention to behavior and outcome through an explicit causal hypothesis with confounders and falsification evidence.
misconceptions: Improved product metrics prove public benefit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Connect intervention to behavior and outcome through an explicit causal hypothesis with confounders and falsification evidence.

competencies.statement: Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon.
misconceptions: Current demand guarantees a durable need.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon.

competencies.statement: Map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users.
misconceptions: Stakeholder importance is proportional to purchasing power.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users.

competencies.statement: Apply public good, harm avoidance, honesty, fairness, privacy, competence, review, and accountability to concrete project conflicts.
misconceptions: Ethics is satisfied by legal compliance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply public good, harm avoidance, honesty, fairness, privacy, competence, review, and accountability to concrete project conflicts.

competencies.statement: Treat disability, language, literacy, device, connectivity, environment, and support needs as core system requirements and research conditions.
misconceptions: Accessibility applies only to the final user interface.

competencies.statement: Define prevention, detection, limit, safe failure, appeal, correction, restitution, communication, and retirement for plausible harms.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define prevention, detection, limit, safe failure, appeal, correction, restitution, communication, and retirement for plausible harms.

competencies.statement: Route domain, legal, accessibility, safety, privacy, or community questions to qualified review and preserve explicit non-claims.

competencies.statement: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.

competencies.statement: Record version, status, jurisdiction, applicability, review date, supersession, and monitoring owner for governing sources.

competencies.statement: Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review.

competencies.statement: Trace source, dependency, data, content, model, font, media, and generated-artifact licenses into distribution obligations.

competencies.statement: Separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions.

competencies.statement: Model inputs, activities, outputs, short and long outcomes, assumptions, external factors, and possible negative pathways.

competencies.statement: Translate each construct into observable measure, population, unit, collection method, timing, uncertainty, and interpretation limit.

competencies.statement: Choose historical, within-subject, benchmark, cohort, staged, qualitative, or build-nothing comparison appropriate to the claim.

competencies.statement: Measure failure, exclusion, burden, privacy, security, accessibility, support, and subgroup differences alongside success.

competencies.statement: Precommit inclusion, exclusion, missing-data, success, stopping, and interpretation rules before seeing outcome data.

competencies.statement: Inventory time, skill, access, authority, data, technology, service, policy, cost, support, and maintenance constraints with owners.

competencies.statement: Design the cheapest ethical experiment that distinguishes outcomes for the highest uncertainty and consequence.

competencies.statement: Estimate development, research, evaluation, accessibility, security, operations, support, contingency, and retirement effort with uncertainty.

competencies.statement: Exercise a real stop, rescope, substitute, or pivot decision when evidence crosses a predeclared threshold.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Exercise a real stop, rescope, substitute, or pivot decision when evidence crosses a predeclared threshold.

competencies.statement: State the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract.

competencies.statement: Order new tasks by prerequisite competence and schedule retrieval of earlier skills before their consequential use.

competencies.statement: Create vertical milestones that retire a named risk, deliver an affected-user signal, and produce assessable evidence.

competencies.statement: Model dependencies, uncertain durations, external waits, integration points, contingency, and explicit scope-release triggers.

competencies.statement: Track accepted evidence, changed assumptions, blocked claims, forecast, decision latency, quality debt, and residual risk rather than percent complete.

competencies.statement: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels.

competencies.statement: Analyze concrete quality-attribute scenarios and trade accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, cost, and simplicity explicitly.

competencies.statement: Define failure domains, resource bounds, dependency timeouts, degraded modes, safe stop, recovery, and blast-radius evidence.

competencies.statement: Plan interface, data, deployment, and organizational evolution with compatibility windows, migration, deprecation, and retirement.

competencies.statement: Run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation.

competencies.statement: Formalize critical identities, states, quantities, relationships, transitions, and impossible conditions in domain language.

competencies.statement: Validate untrusted input for structure, semantics, authorization, limits, normalization, provenance, and version before trusted use.

competencies.statement: State preconditions, invariant, termination, result, counterexample, complexity, scale boundary, and alternative for consequential algorithms.

competencies.statement: Specify operation semantics, status and error taxonomy, idempotency, pagination, consistency, compatibility, rate, privacy, and observation.

competencies.statement: Prove backup, restore, migration, replay, reconciliation, and deletion preserve or intentionally transform domain invariants.

competencies.statement: Trace assets, actors, entry points, data flows, trust boundaries, attack paths, controls, detection, response, and residual risk.

competencies.statement: Test misuse, automation, fraud, harassment, coercion, unsafe advice, resource exhaustion, evasion, and moderator or operator burden.

competencies.statement: Define reporting, triage, severity, containment, remediation, disclosure, dependency response, verification, and learning ownership.

competencies.statement: Build the smallest end-to-end path through authentic interface, domain, data, deployment, telemetry, accessibility, security, and recovery boundaries.

competencies.statement: Reproduce a defect, preserve evidence, compare hypotheses, identify the first invalid transition, repair cause, add regression, and verify changed cases.

competencies.statement: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.

competencies.statement: Wrap external behavior behind owned contracts and test requests, responses, errors, limits, version drift, and semantic mismatch with controlled fixtures.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign effect identity, idempotency, ordering, concurrency, retry, timeout, unknown-outcome, compensation, and reconciliation rules.

competencies.statement: Provide bounded queueing, fallback, cached or reduced behavior, accessible status, safe stop, and recovery when a dependency degrades.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide bounded queueing, fallback, cached or reduced behavior, accessible status, safe stop, and recovery when a dependency degrades.

competencies.statement: Rehearse export, replacement, key rotation, data deletion, contract termination, user communication, and degraded operation without the provider.
misconceptions: An interface abstraction makes vendor exit inexpensive.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Rehearse export, replacement, key rotation, data deletion, contract termination, user communication, and degraded operation without the provider.

competencies.statement: Map behavior, quality, risk, outcome, and impact claims to unit, property, contract, integration, system, fault, human, and production evidence.

competencies.statement: Generate boundary, malformed, hostile, scale, accessibility, concurrency, failure, and environment cases that differ structurally from teaching examples.

competencies.statement: Run representative tasks with neutral protocol, accommodations, observation, success criteria, breakdown capture, consent, and uncertainty.

competencies.statement: Combine automated, keyboard, zoom, contrast, content, screen-reader or structured-text, and disabled-user evidence without unsupported conformance claims.

competencies.statement: Match the strength, population, environment, duration, causality, and certainty of each public claim to the collected evidence.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Match the strength, population, environment, duration, causality, and certainty of each public claim to the collected evidence.

competencies.statement: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty.

competencies.statement: Measure task availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, and support from the user boundary and correlate them with system signals.
misconceptions: Infrastructure health metrics are sufficient service indicators.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure task availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, and support from the user boundary and correlate them with system signals.

competencies.statement: Design logs, metrics, traces, profiles, audits, retention, access, redaction, sampling, cardinality, and cost around diagnostic questions.
misconceptions: Recording complete request context is the safest diagnostic approach.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design logs, metrics, traces, profiles, audits, retention, access, redaction, sampling, cardinality, and cost around diagnostic questions.

competencies.statement: Create symptom, impact, evidence, decision, mitigation, escalation, rollback, recovery, communication, and verification runbooks tested by another operator.
misconceptions: A list of commands is an actionable runbook.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create symptom, impact, evidence, decision, mitigation, escalation, rollback, recovery, communication, and verification runbooks tested by another operator.

competencies.statement: Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure.
misconceptions: Restoring the happy path completes an incident exercise.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure.

competencies.statement: Inventory source, dependencies, tools, generated code, models, data, images, services, licenses, vulnerabilities, and maintainers with current ownership.
misconceptions: A dependency scanner inventories the whole product.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory source, dependencies, tools, generated code, models, data, images, services, licenses, vulnerabilities, and maintainers with current ownership.

competencies.statement: Bind protected source and workflow, isolated build, declared inputs, immutable output, SBOM, provenance, signature or attestation, and verification.
misconceptions: A signed artifact is necessarily trustworthy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind protected source and workflow, isolated build, declared inputs, immutable output, SBOM, provenance, signature or attestation, and verification.

competencies.statement: Move one reviewed artifact through compatible migration, readiness, bounded exposure, guardrails, hold, abort, promotion, and user communication.
misconceptions: Blue-green infrastructure removes deployment risk.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Move one reviewed artifact through compatible migration, readiness, bounded exposure, guardrails, hold, abort, promotion, and user communication.

competencies.statement: Restore into an isolated environment from documented backups, rotate secrets, verify invariants and user workflows, measure recovery objectives, and preserve evidence.
misconceptions: Testing restore over the existing environment proves disaster recovery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Restore into an isolated environment from documented backups, rotate secrets, verify invariants and user workflows, measure recovery objectives, and preserve evidence.

competencies.statement: Plan export, migration, retention, deletion, dependency shutdown, credential revocation, domain and client behavior, communication, and archive evidence.
misconceptions: A capstone can be abandoned after assessment without lifecycle risk.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan export, migration, retention, deletion, dependency shutdown, credential revocation, domain and client behavior, communication, and archive evidence.

competencies.statement: Provide affected-user, operator, maintainer, contributor, reviewer, security, accessibility, and decision-maker documentation at appropriate detail.
misconceptions: One comprehensive README serves every audience well.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide affected-user, operator, maintainer, contributor, reviewer, security, accessibility, and decision-maker documentation at appropriate detail.

competencies.statement: Demonstrate ordinary, changed, boundary, inaccessible-before-repair, failure, recovery, rollback, and stakeholder-outcome cases from the reviewed artifact.
misconceptions: A flawless happy-path demo is the most persuasive evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Demonstrate ordinary, changed, boundary, inaccessible-before-repair, failure, recovery, rollback, and stakeholder-outcome cases from the reviewed artifact.

competencies.statement: Answer unseen causal, counterexample, tradeoff, ethics, accessibility, security, data, operations, and transfer questions using inspectable evidence.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Answer unseen causal, counterexample, tradeoff, ethics, accessibility, security, data, operations, and transfer questions using inspectable evidence.

competencies.statement: Publish selective problem, role, constraints, decisions, implementation, validation, results, limitations, and links without sensitive data or inflated impact.
misconceptions: A portfolio case study should maximize perceived success.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Publish selective problem, role, constraints, decisions, implementation, validation, results, limitations, and links without sensitive data or inflated impact.

competencies.statement: Compare initial predictions with outcomes, explain changed mental models, identify retained misconceptions, and design a harder independent transfer task.
misconceptions: Reflection is a chronological summary of completed work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare initial predictions with outcomes, explain changed mental models, identify retained misconceptions, and design a harder independent transfer task.
